description_long: This object is a single, pale yellow flower with a slender, straight stem. The flower has six broad, slightly overlapping petals arranged in a radial pattern around a central, darker yellow or orange throat. The petals are slightly ruffled at the edges, giving the flower a delicate, natural appearance. The stem is long, thin, and green, with no visible leaves attached. The flower appears to be a daffodil or narcissus, commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or roadside plantings. The overall form is elegant and simple, with a realistic organic texture and subtle color gradients.
description: A pale yellow flower with six broad, ruffled petals and a long, slender green stem. The petals are arranged radially around a darker central throat.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flower with all six petals radiating outward and the central throat visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the stem and the profile of the flower, with petals extending outward and the central throat protruding slightly.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the petals and the attachment point to the stem, with the flower's symmetry evident.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stem and the flower's profile, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with slight overlapping at the base, forming a symmetrical circular pattern around the central throat.
